title: practical english usage - Part 341
content:
Nowadays is an adverb meaning ‘ these days', ‘ at the present time'.

539 nowadays            ___        ____ vocabulary •  544  one of...

For sentences like She’s one of the few women who have/has climbed Everest, ►     130 .1.

not                                or one of my friends (     one my friend   one my friends) Of  cannot be dropped.

one of the/my/those horses (          one of horses) After one of, a noun phrase must have a determiner (e.g. the, my, those).

One of our cats has disappeared. (     On e of our cats have disappeared.) A  following verb is normally singular.

Why don't you ask one of the crew?

Occasionally one of is used with a singular noun referring to a group.

one of our cats (     one of our cat) After one of we  normally use a plural form.

544 one     of  ...

For present perfect instead of future perfect after conjunctions, ►    231.

your test . . ).

Note that we  do not use that after once (     O nce that you've passed Once you know how to ride a bike you never forget it.

Once he had found somewhere to live he started looking for work.

Once you've passed your test I'll let you drive my car.

with a perfect tense.

suggests that something is finished or completed, and is most often used Once can be used as a conjunction, meaning 'after’ , 'as soon as’ . It often 543 once:     conjunction 'Can I have the bill? 'At once, sir!

Note that at once means 'immediately’ .

I'm only going to say this once.

times)’ , it can be used to talk about any time, including the future.

When  once has the more precise meaning of 'one time (not twice or three not        e We must go walking one day. (       W m ust go walking once.) not - Come up and see me sometime. (     Come up and see me once.) Once upon a time there was a beautiful princess.

- I met her once in Venezuela.

To  refer to an indefinite future time, we  can use sometime or one day. Compare: When once means 'at some time’ , we  use it for the past but not for the future.

542 once:     adverb Word  Problems from A  to Z     Section 31 vocabulary' •  545  only: focusing adverbial not Are the banks open this afternoon? (     Are the banks-opened-.. .?) not       anop enedbook / can read you like an open book. (     .. .     .) We   normally use open, not opened, as an adjective.

1 open      and   opened 546 open Only can also be a determiner in a noun phrase (e.g.  You are my only real friend).

For inverted word order after only, as in the last example but one, ►    270.7.

My shoes will only be ready on Friday.

Only then did she realise what she had agreed to.

I saw her only today - she looks much  better.

Only with a time expression can mean 'as recently as’  or ‘ not before’ .

4 only     today,      etc Saturday nights are the only time (that) they play poker.

Poker is the only game (that) they play on Saturday nights.

The  meaning can also be made more precise with a relative structure.

They play poker only on Saturday nights.

They play only poker on Saturday nights.

They only play poker on Saturday nights. (could be ambiguous) refers to. This is generally rather formal. Compare: can be put directly before the object, complement or adverbial expression that it sentences like these from being really ambiguous. However, if necessary only the sentence that only refers to. Even in writing, the context generally stops In speech, the meaning is usually clear because the speaker stresses the part of sister' or ‘ only last night’ .) / only kissed your sister last night. (The sense can be 'only kissed’ , 'only your than one way).

Sometimes sentences with only are ambiguous (they can be understood in more 3 ambiguous        sentences She was only talking like that because she was nervous.

I only like swimming in the sea.  I've only been to India once.

She only reads biographies.    She is only on duty on Tuesdays.

with the verb (►  200 for details).

When  only refers to another part of a sentence, it often goes in 'mid-position' 2 referring       to  other     parts    of  a  sentence Only my mother really understands me.
